reviews.review_msg: Jennifer is an amazing lecturer. Attendance is necessary to understand the content, however lectures are always clear and very interesting. Lots of class discussions and engaging thought experiments, I highly recommend taking a class with her.
Good teacher, awesome grading

She's absolutely amazing professor , I absolutely love her. I wished she taught something other than philosophy so that i could that that course as well. To get a good grade in her class , you only need to focus on what she says , makes notes and READ THE TEXTBOOK. You can easily get A- and above in this course if you engage with her well.

Jennifer is very understanding, friendly, and teaches very well. However, classes sometimes don't feel very engaging even though she tries to keep it interesting. At times her explanations may not seem very clear. Overall, if you read the text and her slides you will do well.
Jennifer is very patient and her tests are fair. Make sure you study hard then you will definitely be fine in the exams. Don't hesitate to attend her office hours when you need help!

Her lecture and powerpoint slide is clear and easy to understand. Just focus on what she said in the class and you will be fine. All of her tests are mostly taken from class examples so you shouldn't skip the class. Working hard because her exams are pretty tricky.

The exams are quite tricky. You need to be clear of concepts and read the textbook to get a really good grade. Jennifer is good at explaining all the concepts and paying attention in her class does half the job.
Best one for Critical Thinking. Clear lectures and friendly. She also do explain detailedly about all ur questions. Just be aware of all those tricks in exams and a easy A.

Lectures are not clear. Does not know how to articulate her knowledge. Poor lectures. Powerpoints are unclear and boring. Not much homework though. Easy grader. Doesn't challenge you at all. If you just want a course to pass and don't care about learning take her class. Did not like her at all! Shuts down discussions, short temper. Judgemental. F
She has magic power that can attract your attention. I love her.
Good.

Jennifer is an amazing instructor. She is inspiring and her exam is fair. Some necessary but not sufficient tips to perform well: Do not miss the class. Do the preview before the class. Listen to her seriously. Before the exam try to memorize and understand the "review" part each chapter. While doing the exam be much careful and cautious. Cheers.

Jennifer is a wonderful instructor and I miss the time learning with her. If you are hesitating choosing the instructor for PHIL105, I highly highly recommend you to choose her. For final I did not perform perfectly and made a lot of mistakes. An A is lucky and satisfactory. But today is the grade releasing day and it's A+. Thank you Jennifer :)

Jennifer is an amazing instructor and she deserves a higher (at least above 4.5) evaluation I think. Clear and interesting class. She is caring and glad to answer your questions. For this logic course I think understanding is the most important. Her test is not much hard but needs you to be really careful and earnest. Read her PPT slides repeatedly
She is one of the best philosophy instructors. Clear and nice. Love to study with her.
really clear concepts, nice. just concentrate in class so exams will be okay.
Best instructor ever. I love her.
Best instructor ever.
Nice.
Good professor. Lectures are interesting. Don't let that fool you...  Exams are v tough..  Negative marking for complicated Multiple choice questions..  65% is C and the class average was 60%
Jennifer present clear lectures  using  vivid examples.
Make most course materials become almost common sense, not much study required

She really enjoys teaching this course therefore her positive attitude and detailed knowledge of the course content makes the lectures a great experience. Sadly the course was at night time so we ended class early a few times. She gives great feed back and is more than willing to go over content you don't understand.
Very Irritating Teacher
one of the best and nicest professor ever. I'd suggest to take a class with her.
She's a lovely person who is eager to help. Marking is pretty tough.

History of ethics is interesting when taught clearly, organized and straight forward. Jennifer was pretty disorganized and unprepared in this class and presented some of the material very intuitively and speculatively. Her feedback was not helpful, reflecting her teaching method, namely confusing and obscure. Marking was not great either.
Her Nietzsche course was the best I've ever taken (and probably ever will) at SFU. She's a fairly tough marker, but fair nonetheless and offers useful criticism. TAKE THIS COURSE!

Fantastic prof! Loved her class, she is engaging, knows the material inside and out, and is very good about responding to student questions. She was available for extra help, gave useful feedback on drafts to our papers, and was a very fair marker. Definitely going to take another class of hers if I can.
Makes philosophy relevant.
Amazing. Very knowledgable. Highly recommend.
Great.
I really liked this course.  Took it a few years ago and still remember some of the lectures.
Great course, good lectures, but it's definitely not easy...
This prof has turned me off from ever taking another philosophy class.

This was one of the worst classes I have taken so far at SFU. I had high hopes going into this class but that quickly faded. The prof was unorganized and unclear in terms of the material that she covered.  She was a very difficult marker. I would NOT recommend this class to anyone!

tried to go for help, only had one day of office hours, considering all of the classes she teaches one would think she would have more, she's nice though however I do not recommend this course to be taken for a first year's first semester.

She is one of my favourite profs at SFU.  I always saw her for my papers and she gave lots of suggestions.  If you see her for your papers, you should do fine.  Also do the study guide she gives you since everything except for the essay is taken from there.  Don't re-read the textbook, just do the study guide and you'll be fine!!!
Her lectures are extremely helpful, she makes it really easy to understand the material covered. The final was super easy.

This is my second philosophy course that I took and let me say that she's an awesome prof.  I saw her for all of my papers and she was very helpful every single time.  If she gives out a study guide, DO IT.  The questions from the exams come from there.  There isn't a lot of guessing.
If you're looking for an easy course, this probably isn't it.  She's a really good lecturer.

If u are not interested in philosophy, nothing is going to be fun in this class. But the only good thing is that she is predicable in the terms of her marking and her essay questions even though she does mark hard. The overheads aren't bad, and as long as you do the readings before class it isn't too bad and you should be able to follow along.
Her Nietzsche course was one of the best courses that I've ever taken.

She's is a nice professor, tries to get the class going but only those who actually get it or those who speak up more often will participate. Notes are in overhead format and are very unorganized. Often goes off topic, analogies and examples are difficult to comprehend.  Textbook is expensive, heavy and barely used. Worst of all it was at night,ZZZ
I took one 200 and one 300 level course with her.  If I had the opportunity to take more courses with her, I definitely would have.

To previous poster: I'm a girl and I certainly did not get an A on the midterm though I'm in my 4rth year and usually do get A's. So your sexism theory can't be totally correct. Furthermore, for the essays she will mark them blind--she asks us not to put our name on them to ensure a lack of bias. I love this, because mostly profs let bias reign.

I'm not sure if I have the course # right, but I took her NIetzsche course.  It was fabulous.  One of the best courses that I've ever taken.  It's not easy, but it's also not really hard.  Just right, I'd say.  The lectures were interesting and fun.

The prof is fine, shes friendly. I went to her office one time and pretty much told me to redo the paper entirely. She knows the useless stuff. Don't take this class unless your witting skills are excellent. You will not learn anything useful here and discussion is for those who see the world twisted. eg) the chair you're sitting on does not exist.
Great course.  I really enjoyed the lectures.

All I have to say is I learned so much in this course.  The most valuable thing I learned in her course was how to count. I would sit and count how many times it was possible for someone say "What is justice...", "In so far as". Yes, once I counted 35 times!
Good course, but you must attend lecture if you want to do well.  She is able to explain complex ideas very clearly.  Really enjoyed this class.

She is very nice, but very disorganized.  She was late almost every day, and twice she cancelled class at the last second.  Every time a question was asked she could not give a very convincing answer, didn't seem as knowledgable as most professors.
This is a really enjoyable course.
I took Nietzsche with her and few years back and absolutely loved it.  She is very passionate about the material.  The class was great.  If it's offered again-take it.

I didn't have any particular interest in philosophy before this course, but, I must say, this course really sparked an interest.  The prof. is very enthusiastic and is able to explain complex material in a clear and simple manner.  Overall, great!

Interesting story: the first essay I worked myself silly, got it proofread by my friend, and think it was one of the best papers I wrote (I'm an A-student, mind). Got a B-.  (Lowest EVER on a philosophy essay.)  For the midterm: hardly studied, spewed BS on the questions, my answers were vague and disjointed.  Got a B+.  Hmmm.

Wow, I thought that 320 was great.  Don't know what that previous person was talking about.  If you haven't done the reading, you might find that she moves too quickly.  Aside from that, it was great and so was she!  Very interesting course!
Disorganized, unclear, unhelpful, went too fast...did everything a good professor doesn't.
really good prof.. explained the material well and showed lots of enthusiasm (for nietszche)

Very nice person.  For 300 classes she needs to lose the overheads, they seem to mess her up.  She will follow them, then get into something, then look back at them and read them out.  This really loses momentum.     When it comes to the material she knows the detail.  What she needs to focus on is preperation for student questions.
This was an interesting course.  Jennifer is clear and helfpul.  Uses overhead notes and emails them to the class.

She is a really nice instructor.  However, she is disorganized, does not respond quickly to emails, and is a slow marker.  But the class discussions are enjoyable and thought provoking.  If you are patient then I reccomend this class.

Jenn has good notes, assigns good readings, and a good way of teaching the course.  However she does get side-tracked a lot by questions, but that's most likely due to the first year students.  I would def. take another class with her, just not with the amount of repetition i found between philosophy 100 and 151 at SFU - honestly, 2/3rds the same!
Jennifer made this course very interesting.  She was very fair.  I'd take another course with her.
Passionate, dedicated and helpful but new, so a bit disorganized and a bit thrown off by students going outside her notes or views. she has a lot of potential and i would definitely take her again.

Most disorganized prof ever. She loses track of what she's talking about and goes off on unrelated tangents regularly. Changes reading assignments and due dates last second and only notifies via email. Honestly, nice person (she brought us cookies on Hallowe'en) but the worst prof I've ever had. At least her class was really easy.
somewhat disorganized but otherwise a good prof.. i think she is new to teaching, or at least sfu, so she'll likely get better.

She's really nice and seems to care about her students doing well.  The lectures got better as the semester progressed, but there was a lot of jumping around between texts and she moves at a quick pace.  Her essays and exams rely a lot on what is said in class, however, to get a mark higher than a B you must go beyond what is discussed in class.

She's a good teacher and she cares about teaching, but students in the class throw her off easily.  Her teaching style is also overheads, but she switches them too fast and it's confusing.  Otherwise, she's great.
she is very focussed on the students and is always willing to help
No Comments

she's super helpful if you see her outside of class. her essays are thought-provoking and she's a fair marker.  she takes attendence every class and her essays depend heavily on notes given in class. i think she'll be the one of the more favored profs in a couple of years, she's just fine tuning her teaching skills, but she's got great potential.

She's the departments blonde bombshell, lol. Super nice and friendly. She's new so she looses control of the class from time to time, but she'll get better. She marks fairly and is really lax about dead lines. Her essay topics are also pretty easy, and the amount of reading she assigns is fair.
